name: Crimea
flavor_text: Crimea lives in political limbo — occupied, contested, and exhausted. Half the population identifies with Russia, half with Ukraine. Both sides apply pressure. The front lines are 300 kilometers away but close enough that artillery is audible on quiet nights.

advisors.name: Viktor Sorokin
advisors.agenda: Secure ruble-based financing while quietly maintaining euro-compatible accounts.

briefing: Western sanctions block most banking. We operate on cash and cryptocurrency.
Tourism revenue collapsed after 2022. The hotels are empty.
Moscow subsidies arrive late and with strings attached.
disagreement: That spending will get us flagged by Moscow auditors.
We can't run a budget when half our transactions are blocked.
roast: We're an economic island. Every choice is a workaround.
I manage money that can't move. Impressive, in a terrible way.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been maintaining a parallel account for Ukrainian authorities. Just in case.

advisors.name: Col. Dmitri Volkov
advisors.agenda: Maintain military cooperation with Russian forces and integration of peninsula defense.

briefing: Russian forces require expanded logistics support from civilian infrastructure.
Ukrainian drone activity over the Kerch bridge is increasing.
Conscription pressure from Moscow is coming. Prepare for it.
disagreement: You resist Moscow and invite consequences none of us want.
Civilian comfort is secondary to military security here.
roast: Your governance delays military readiness. That is unacceptable.
Moscow is watching every decision you make.
dialogue.betrayal: I've reported your disloyalty to Moscow command. Expect a visit.

advisors.name: Anna Kovalenko
advisors.agenda: Maintain Crimean infrastructure against war damage and sanctions-driven shortages.

briefing: The water supply from mainland Ukraine was cut. Desalination covers 60%.
Road infrastructure requires Russian equipment we can't import under sanctions.
Sevastopol residential zones near the naval base need evacuation planning.
disagreement: You can't build anything lasting while the war defines the political reality.
Military priorities keep consuming the infrastructure budget.
roast: The peninsula is decaying. I patch holes while the house burns.
Infrastructure planning for a warzone. My career has taken a turn.
dialogue.betrayal: I've submitted my resignation to both Moscow and Kyiv. I'm done with this.

advisors.name: Stepan Morozov
advisors.agenda: Keep the Kerch Bridge operational and maintain alternative supply routes.

briefing: The bridge is the lifeline. Without it, we're a ship taking on water.
Ferry alternatives exist but carry 20% of bridge capacity.
Fuel supply is intermittent. Military gets priority; civilians get remainder.
disagreement: Closing that route for maintenance during active operations is reckless.
roast: The bridge has been hit twice. My supply plans last three days.
I route convoys around craters. This is my life now.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been coordinating with Ukrainian logistics to preserve supply chains. Someone had to.

advisors.name: Mufti Ismet Gafarov
advisors.agenda: Protect Crimean Tatar rights and community identity under Russian administration.

briefing: Tatar property seizures continue. Moscow says it's administrative. It's ethnic.
Our mosques are monitored. Our language is suppressed.
The Tatar community still has pro-Ukrainian sentiment. That's not going away.
disagreement: You collaborate with policies that erase my people.
Neutrality in oppression is not neutral.
roast: The Tatars have been exiled before. This feels familiar.
You govern us without hearing us.
dialogue.betrayal: I've formally requested international protection for the Tatar community. Your government is the threat.

decisions.title: Crimean Tatar Rights Policy
decisions.description: Moscow has ordered further restrictions on Tatar cultural institutions. The Tatar community is the largest indigenous group and has international legal protections. Complying loses local loyalty; resisting risks Moscow's reaction.

options.label: Comply with Moscow's order
options.description: Full enforcement. Tatar community loses trust permanently.

options.label: Selective enforcement with protections
options.description: Partially comply. Balance Moscow and community.

options.label: Quietly obstruct enforcement
options.description: Bureaucratically delay. Moscow suspects; community benefits.

decisions.title: Water Crisis Response
decisions.description: Ukraine cut the North Crimean Canal. Desalination covers 60% of needs. A new plant would solve the shortfall but requires 80M and imported equipment under sanctions.

options.label: Build desalination plant via gray-market imports
options.description: Circumvent sanctions. Solves water crisis, legal risk.

options.label: Aggressive water rationing
options.description: Extend current supply. Painful but no sanctions risk.

options.label: Request Moscow emergency water transfer
options.description: Petition Russia for supply. Politically dependent solution.

decisions.title: Moscow Conscription Demand
decisions.description: Moscow has issued a conscription quota for Crimea. The order requires 15,000 men. Ukrainian-identified residents refuse. Tatar community is vocal in opposition. Non-compliance risks Moscow sanctions against the regional government.

options.label: Full compliance
options.description: Enforce the quota. Community backlash severe.

options.label: Partial compliance with exemptions
options.description: Report partial quota. Plausible deniability.

options.label: Administrative delay
options.description: Paperwork the process. Buys months of friction.

crises.title: Kerch Bridge Structural Damage
crises.description: A Ukrainian strike has damaged two spans of the Kerch Bridge. Supply chains are broken. Moscow is furious. The peninsula faces shortages within two weeks.

options.label: Emergency ferry system activation
options.description: Maximize sea transport. 30% of bridge capacity.

options.label: Rationing and stockpile activation
options.description: Restrict civilian supply. Military priority maintained.

options.label: Appeal for emergency Moscow airlift
options.description: Request Russian military logistics support.

crises.title: Mass Tatar Detention
crises.description: FSB has arrested 200 Tatars in a sweeping anti-terrorism operation. International bodies are condemning. The evidence is thin. Mufti Gafarov is demanding you intervene.

options.label: Formally request FSB review
options.description: Petition for legal review. Politically risky with Moscow.

options.label: Publicly support the operation
options.description: Endorse FSB. Community trust destroyed.

options.label: Stay silent publicly, provide legal aid quietly
options.description: Navigate carefully. Helps some, commits to neither side.

crises.title: Drone Strike on Civilian Area
crises.description: A Ukrainian drone struck a residential block in Simferopol. Eight dead. Russian media is amplifying. The population wants protection and answers.

options.label: Demand Moscow increase air defense
options.description: Formally petition Russian military for S-400 coverage.

options.label: Evacuate high-risk zones
options.description: Move civilians from military-adjacent areas.

options.label: Public memorial and resilience messaging
options.description: Mourn and move forward narrative.

crises.title: Administration Defection Wave
crises.description: Seven senior officials have fled to Ukrainian-controlled territory. Moscow suspects your loyalties. An FSB investigator has arrived with questions.

options.label: Cooperate fully with FSB investigation
options.description: Open your office to Moscow. Keeps your position, loses staff trust.

options.label: Conduct your own parallel review
options.description: Demonstrate control. Moscow will check your work.

options.label: Publicly blame Ukrainian intelligence
options.description: Narrative deflection. Satisfies Moscow, plausible to locals.

scandals.title: Dual Budget Ledger Exposed
scandals.description: An official was found maintaining two budgets — one for Moscow, one for actual expenditure.

scandals.title: Seized Property Profit
scandals.description: Officials profited personally from Ukrainian property seizures.

scandals.title: Conscription Exemption Sale
scandals.description: Families paid officials to remove men from conscription lists.

positive: Maybe things can improve. We'll see.
A decision that helps actual people. Rare.
This is what governance should look like.
Small progress in a complicated situation.
Water in the taps again. I didn't think I'd see the day.
Even under sanctions, they found a way. I give them that.
My pension came on time this month. First time in a year.
They actually listened. To us, not to Moscow. That's something.
neutral: Between Russia and Ukraine, we just live.
The war is far. But not far enough.
Simferopol looks the same. People go to market. Life goes on somehow.
The Kerch Bridge is open today. Yesterday it wasn't. You plan around it.
Moscow says one thing, Kyiv says another. We stopped listening to both.
My neighbor has a Ukrainian passport, I have a Russian one. We still share bread.
negative: I don't know who to be loyal to anymore.
Half my family is on the other side of the front line.
Our governor serves Moscow, not Crimea.
The Tatars were here before any of this. No one cares.
They took my cousin for the army. He didn't want to go. He had no choice.
The canal was our water. They blocked it, we suffer. Politics kills people slowly.
The hotels in Yalta are empty. This used to be a resort. Now it's a garrison.
FSB at the door again. Third time this month. I'm not even political.
crisis: Every crisis, we're the ones who suffer.
War came to us. We didn't choose this.
No one is coming to save Crimea.
Another explosion. Another night without sleep.
The bridge is down again. We're an island. A real one this time.
200 Tatars arrested and the world just watches. This is ethnic cleansing in slow motion.
Drone over Simferopol last night. My children sleep in the corridor now.
No water, no bridge, no signal. We're cut off from everything.
scandal: They steal no matter which flag flies.
At least the old corruption was local. This is imported.
I'm disgusted. With all of them.
Paid 3,000 rubles to get my son off the draft list. I'm ashamed. I'd do it again.
Ukrainian house, Russian bureaucrat, same stolen furniture. Nothing changed.
The seized dachas in Yalta are being rented by officials on holiday. I saw them.
Two ledgers. Two flags. One pocket. Classic Crimea.
media: CRIMEA.REALITIES: Water rationing enters week 3 in Dzhankoi — desalination still 40% short of demand
RIA NOVOSTI KRYM: Governor praises Moscow subsidies, calls peninsula 'model of stability'
KRYM.REALII (@KrymRealii): FSB arrests sweep Bakhchysarai — families confirm no charges filed #TatarLives
CRIMEAN TATAR TV (QHA): 'Our mosques are monitored, our language disappears' — Mufti Gafarov speaks
SEVASTOPOL MEDIA: Naval base expansion displaces 400 residents in Inkerman district
UA:PERSHYI: Kerch Bridge damage confirmed — Russian state media silent on structural assessment
KRYM.REALII: Drone strike on Simferopol residential block — 8 dead, no air defense response
INTERFAX-UKRAINE: Sanctions tighten — gray market imports of construction equipment surge through Turkey
politician: Head of Republic of Crimea: 'The peninsula is Russia, was Russia, and will be Russia. This is settled history.'
Sevastopol Governor Razvozhaev: 'Air defense systems will be reinforced. The enemy will not intimidate us.'
State Duma Deputy (Crimean seat): 'Any obstruction of conscription quotas is sabotage of the war effort.'
Kremlin spokesperson Peskov: 'The situation in Crimea is stable. Reports to the contrary are enemy disinformation.'
Verkhovna Rada (Kyiv): 'We recognize all Crimean residents as Ukrainian citizens under temporary occupation.'
Mejlis of Crimean Tatar People (in exile, Kyiv): 'The arrests are political terror. We demand ICC intervention.'
Russian Civic Chamber rep: 'The Tatar community is protected under Russian law. Allegations are fabricated.'
Ukrainian MFA: 'Conscription of Ukrainian citizens in occupied Crimea is a war crime under Geneva Conventions.'
activist: Crimean Solidarity (Bakhchysarai): 'Biz burdamiz — we are here. They can arrest us, they cannot erase us.'
Human Rights Watch field report: Crimean Tatars face systematic detention, property seizure, and mosque raids.
Memorial International (pre-ban records): Over 200 political prisoners from Crimea documented since 2014.
Ukrainian Helsinki Human Rights Union: 'The North Crimean Canal closure is a collective punishment tactic.'
Crimean Tatar Resource Center: 'Our children are taught in Russian. Our language is dying in a single generation.'
Amnesty International: 'FSB's anti-terrorism operations in Crimea disproportionately target ethnic Tatars.'
No Conscription Crimea network: 'Allah qorsun bu savaşı — God curse this war. Our men are not Russia's soldiers.'
Mediazona: At least 11 Crimean journalists jailed or exiled since 2014. Independent press is dead on the peninsula.
street: Yok artık biz burada. They take our sons, our land, our mosques. Allah qorsun. (Tatar elder, Bakhchysarai)
My pension doubled when Russia came. Don't ask me to regret it. I'm 72. (Russian settler, Simferopol)
I have a Ukrainian passport under my floor. My Russian one is in my wallet. You learn to live double. (local)
Tatarlar her zaman burada oldu. We were here before Catherine, before Stalin, before Putin. Biz burdamiz.
The Kerch Bridge? I cross it in my dreams. In real life I haven't left in 18 months. (pensioner, Kerch)
Water three hours a day. I have five buckets. The regime has press conferences. (resident, Dzhankoi)
My brother went to the front in '22. Haven't heard from him since March. Not Ukrainian, not Russian. Just gone.
Yalta without tourists is just a sad city next to a beautiful sea. That's us now. (hotel worker, Yalta)

scandal_reactions.minor: Authorities have confirmed the situation is being monitored.
scandal_reactions.moderate: The situation is sensitive given current political context. Tread carefully.
scandal_reactions.major: Both domestic and external parties are watching this closely.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: Leadership change is being discussed. This could accelerate it significantly.

romance_exposure.flavour: State media buries the story. Everyone already knows.
